title: Boat Rental in Los Roques: A 2026 Guide to Island Hopping by Boat
html:
Set off from Gran Roque to explore the Los Roques archipelago by private boat, with prices starting at around 250 EUR for a half-day RIB rental. BoatHire24 makes it easy to compare options and secure your chosen vessel for a day of Caribbean adventure.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Los Roques?

Daily rental rates range from 250 EUR for a small motorboat to over 4,000 EUR for a crewed luxury yacht. A shared tour with up to 12 guests might cost 80 EUR per person, while a private catamaran for a full day starts around 1,200 EUR. For the latest availability and to browse boats for your dates, compare verified listings on BoatHire24.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Los Roques?

No licence is required to charter a boat with a skipper, but bareboat rentals typically require a recognised boating licence. The operator will confirm specific requirements when you book, as rules vary by vessel size and engine power. For peace of mind, always verify with the charter company before arrival.

Where to set off in Los Roques

The archipelago centres on Gran Roque, the only town and main departure point. Its sheltered harbour, lined with small docks and fishing pangas, offers easy boarding for day trips. From there, you can head to Cayo de Agua, Francisquí, or Madrisquí, all known for sandbars and shallow anchorages. Other popular stops include Noronquí and Crasquí, where you can swim and picnic. Most marinas are simple moorings rather than full-service ports, so fuel and water are best arranged on Gran Roque.

Which boat to rent in Los Roques

  1. RIB (rigid inflatable boat), best for speed and accessing shallow cays; seats 6, 8 guests; from 350 EUR half-day.
  2. Small motorboat (e.g., Boston Whaler), ideal for private fishing or island-hopping; up to 6 people; from 400 EUR full day.
  3. Catamaran (e.g., Lagoon 42), stable and roomy for families or groups; sleeps 8 day guests; from 1,200 EUR full day.
  4. Crewed luxury yacht (e.g., Princess F70), maximum comfort with skipper and crew; up to 12 guests; from 3,000 EUR per day. You can view the Princess F70 for hire on BoatHire24 for a premium experience.

Best time to visit Los Roques by boat

High season runs from December to April, when trade winds bring steady breeze and clear skies. The prevailing wind, the Alisio, typically blows from the east at 15, 20 knots, making for excellent sailing.

For a quieter trip, consider May or November; the water is warm year-round and anchorages are less crowded outside peak months.
Prices may drop by 20-30% in low season, but always check ahead for weather windows.

At a glance

  • Price band: 250, 4,500 EUR per day
  • Peak months: December to April
  • Trip length: Half-day to full week
  • Capacity: 2, 12 guests per boat
  • Currency: EUR (widely accepted, with some US dollars)
  • Closest international airport: El Gran Roque Airport (shares island)

Questions about boat rental in Los Roques

Can I rent a boat for just a few hours in Los Roques?

Yes, many operators offer half-day trips of four to five hours, especially to nearby cays like Cayo de Agua, which is about 5 nautical miles from Gran Roque.

What is the best boat for a family with children?

A catamaran or RIB with shaded seating is recommended, as they provide stability and easy boarding from shallow beaches for children.

Are there any all-inclusive charter options?

Several companies offer full-day crewed charters that include lunch, drinks, and snorkelling gear, usually priced from 800 EUR for a group of six.

How far in advance should I book a boat in Los Roques for 2026?

It is wise to book at least two to three months ahead for peak season (December to April) to secure your preferred vessel and dates.
